## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Discrete contract reshape — yoke domain shifts vessel→reliquary. Mechanically intricate but design is locked. Bundled orphan vessel cleanup closes the `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` test thread carrying-forward since BBAA3.
  
  ## Locked decisions
  
  - `rbw-dY` channel: `param1` → `""` (no folio); operation iterates every vessel under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*/` with rbrv.env, writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into each
  - Primitive does **not** git commit — leaves changed files in repo dir
  - Orchestrator (inscribe-reliquary case in onboarding-sequence) **must** commit at end-of-case
  - Frontispiece: `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels`
  - BCG-compliant unimpeachable error handling — pre-validate reliquary stamp before touching any vessel; vessel-write failures surface clear diagnostic identifying which vessel failed and which were already written
  - Orphan vessel cleanup (bundled): delete `rbev-busybox-airgap-negative-canary`, `rbev-busybox-native-verify`, `rbev-sentry-deb-airgap`, `rbev-ubu-safety`
  
  ## Done criteria
  
  - `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` passes; fast suite 26/26
  - `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` green
  - Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
  - All callers of `rbw-dY` updated; spec, tabtarget filename, zipper enrollment, frontispiece consistent
  
  ## Discovery recipes
  
  - Caller audit: `grep -rn "rbw-dY\|RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY\|YokesReliquaryInVessel" Tools/ tt/`
  - Per-vessel yoke-list residue in onboarding: `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const and consuming call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`
  - Spec: `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` — rewrite to reliquary-domain semantics
  - Witness probe: `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` may need adjustment if witness vessel is now wildcard-discovered
  - Tabtarget rename: `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh` (filesystem rename + executable bit)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Validator (`rbrv_regime.sh:48`) mode-awareness rework — wildcard-yoke renders this latent, not load-bearing
  - Orphan vessel restoration — these directories are deleted, not preserved

Adding a second module's worth of magic strings would have created a duplicate-constants smell across rbtdrp_/rbtdrc_, so consolidated by extraction: defined `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` and `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` in rbtdri_invocation.rs (the natural home: rbtdri_invoke_global is the primitive that propagates env-pairs to tabtargets, and rbtdri_invocation.rs already exports RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR and RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY using the same pattern). Updated rbtdrc_crucible.rs (6 sites) and rbtdrp_pristine.rs (1 site at line 1025; was line 1030 before the dead-constant deletion) to import and use the new shared constants. Removed the rbtdrp_-private duplicates (4 lines including the docstring) since the shared rbtdri_ versions now serve as the single source of truth across the rbtd crate. Bash assignment-prefix `VAR=value func` is the language's primitive for scoped env override and is symmetric with the dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec` (buz_zipper.sh:280) — same primitive at finer scope. Preserves BBAAs's universal-BUZ_FOLIO-contract design intent (handlers read BUZ_FOLIO uniformly across all rb*_, jjfp_, buhw_, rbgg_ modules; cli.sh furnishes also read BUZ_FOLIO for kindle); the rbob → rbfd internal calls just needed to honor the contract by setting BUZ_FOLIO to rbob's natural-derivative (RBRN_*_VESSEL sigil) for the inner call. After this patch, BUZ_FOLIO usage is uniform: dispatcher writes once via exec, callers may scope-override via assignment-prefix, all consumers read. Zero handler/dispatcher edits needed. (B) Strip --force flag from rbfl_jettison and rbfl_abjure plus their RBSIJ/RBSAA specs and 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs — origin trace: docket revision 260128-2116 for ₢APAAT (silks `add-image-delete-by-ark`) introduced 'Confirm before deletion or require --force flag' as freelance docket text never operator-authorized; subsequent reslating preserved it; alignment commit 525b7fac (`:A:` Feb 9 2026) pulled `(add confirmation/force)` into the spec; bd344f92 (Haiku sub-agent, same day) implemented z_force/z_skip_confirm/--force doc-param mechanically; 388dd454 carried it through the rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh extraction; 7167b795 (BBAAs) included `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift specifically because of --force. Removed: `local z_force="${1:-}"`, `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."`, `local z_skip_confirm=false` + `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch, and the `if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"` wrapping around `buc_require` — `buc_require` runs unconditionally (single coherent path). The codebase already had a non-load-bearing-correct skip mechanism (`BURE_CONFIRM=skip` env var checked at buc_require:335-338, buc_command.sh) — --force was a redundant duplicate variant. Updated all 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs (lines 2603, 2688, 2720, 2820, 2948, 3003) from `&[&hallmark, "--force"], &[]` to `&[&hallmark], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` — fixtures continue to auto-confirm via the existing env-var bypass; --force literal disappears from the entire rbk tree (verified via grep). Spec edits: RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:51-59 collapses 'Unless --force flag is provided' nested-step into unconditional 'Confirm Jettison' step; RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc:84-92 same shape. Caveat: scoped to the empirical failure trace and the unauthorized-flag scrub; if any handler outside this bug class has a similar internal-caller mismatch, will surface on the next empirical run as a new failure to diagnose.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.                    
768cc71c  ₣BB       BCG: codify executable-bit rule — _cli.sh must be +x, all other module *.sh must not. Adds Filesystem marker subsection to § CLI as Module Gateway, drops 'executable' from the line 41 file-map (now stated separately as a hard rule), and adds matching item to Module Maturity Checklist § content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113

124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAA6

## Character

Discrete contract reshape — yoke domain shifts vessel→reliquary. Mechanically intricate but design is locked. Bundled orphan vessel cleanup closes the `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` test thread carrying-forward since BBAA3.

## Locked decisions

- `rbw-dY` channel: `param1` → `""` (no folio); operation iterates every vessel under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*/` with rbrv.env, writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into each
- Primitive does **not** git commit — leaves changed files in repo dir
- Orchestrator (inscribe-reliquary case in onboarding-sequence) **must** commit at end-of-case
- Frontispiece: `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels`
- BCG-compliant unimpeachable error handling — pre-validate reliquary stamp before touching any vessel; vessel-write failures surface clear diagnostic identifying which vessel failed and which were already written
- Orphan vessel cleanup (bundled): delete `rbev-busybox-airgap-negative-canary`, `rbev-busybox-native-verify`, `rbev-sentry-deb-airgap`, `rbev-ubu-safety`

## Done criteria

- `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` passes; fast suite 26/26
- `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` green
- Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
- All callers of `rbw-dY` updated; spec, tabtarget filename, zipper enrollment, frontispiece consistent

## Discovery recipes

- Caller audit: `grep -rn "rbw-dY\|RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY\|YokesReliquaryInVessel" Tools/ tt/`
- Per-vessel yoke-list residue in onboarding: `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const and consuming call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`
- Spec: `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` — rewrite to reliquary-domain semantics
- Witness probe: `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` may need adjustment if witness vessel is now wildcard-discovered
- Tabtarget rename: `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh` (filesystem rename + executable bit)

## Out of scope

- Validator (`rbrv_regime.sh:48`) mode-awareness rework — wildcard-yoke renders this latent, not load-bearing
- Orphan vessel restoration — these directories are deleted, not preserved

7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Discrete contract reshape — yoke domain shifts vessel→reliquary. Mechanically intricate but design is locked. Bundled orphan vessel cleanup closes the `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` test thread carrying-forward since BBAA3.
  
  ## Locked decisions
  
  - `rbw-dY` channel: `param1` → `""` (no folio); operation iterates every vessel under `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*/` with rbrv.env, writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into each
  - Primitive does **not** git commit — leaves changed files in repo dir
  - Orchestrator (inscribe-reliquary case in onboarding-sequence) **must** commit at end-of-case
  - Frontispiece: `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels`
  - BCG-compliant unimpeachable error handling — pre-validate reliquary stamp before touching any vessel; vessel-write failures surface clear diagnostic identifying which vessel failed and which were already written
  - Orphan vessel cleanup (bundled): delete `rbev-busybox-airgap-negative-canary`, `rbev-busybox-native-verify`, `rbev-sentry-deb-airgap`, `rbev-ubu-safety`
  
  ## Done criteria
  
  - `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` passes; fast suite 26/26
  - `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` green
  - Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`
  - All callers of `rbw-dY` updated; spec, tabtarget filename, zipper enrollment, frontispiece consistent
  
  ## Discovery recipes
  
  - Caller audit: `grep -rn "rbw-dY\|RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY\|YokesReliquaryInVessel" Tools/ tt/`
  - Per-vessel yoke-list residue in onboarding: `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const and consuming call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`
  - Spec: `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc` — rewrite to reliquary-domain semantics
  - Witness probe: `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` may need adjustment if witness vessel is now wildcard-discovered
  - Tabtarget rename: `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `tt/rbw-dY.DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh` (filesystem rename + executable bit)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Validator (`rbrv_regime.sh:48`) mode-awareness rework — wildcard-yoke renders this latent, not load-bearing
  - Orphan vessel restoration — these directories are deleted, not preserved

91a75146  ₢BBAAg    Final assembly of the gauntlet test suite — rbw-tP release-qualification entry point. Composes the 11-fixture gauntlet ladder (enrollment-validation → pristine-lifecycle → canonical-establish → onboarding-sequence → regime-validation → regime-smoke → four-mode → tadmor → moriah → srjcl → pluml) as a TestSuite-shaped composition over existing infrastructure: ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET bash array in rbte_engine.sh + gauntlet) resolver branch + tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh launcher. The bash for-loop in rbte_suite() under set -e provides the suite-level fail-fast contract for free; no orchestrator-script needed. rbw-tP wired via rbz_zipper RBZ_QUALIFY_PRISTINE enrollment + rbq_qualify_pristine handler, which delegates contract enforcement to pristine-lifecycle case 1 (the entry-contract gate landed in BBAAB checks tree-clean + RBRR-blank + RBRA-absent + nameplate-hallmarks-empty + vessel-depot-fields-empty in one aggregated diagnostic). Docket prescribed dual enforcement at the rbw-tP layer plus pristine-lifecycle case 1, but BBAAB's final landed implementation explicitly omitted a commit-signature primitive ("no signature trailer") and the 5 violation classes are already enforced exhaustively as the gauntlet's first content fixture; duplicating them in bash would re-implement Rust predicates for cosmetic earlier-failure with maintenance-drift hazard. Operator running rbw-tP without marshal-zero sees enrollment-validation pass quickly (state-indifferent preflight) then pristine-lifecycle case 1 surface the violation diagnostic. Moriah included as a tadmor twin per operator direction: registered RBTDRM_FIXTURE_MORIAH + RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH sharing RBTDRC_SECTIONS_SECURITY (renamed from RBTDRC_SECTIONS_TADMOR) — runtime semantics identical to tadmor; provenance differs (cloud-built airgap vs local kludge). Section names stripped of "tadmor-" prefix to make the array genuinely shareable: 8 sections renamed (basic-infra, ifrit-attacks, observation, correlated, sortie-attacks, unilateral-novel, coordinated-attacks, coordinated-integrity). Theurge-ifrit-context doc table updated with new prefix-free names plus prose noting tadmor+moriah share the array. Build clean under #![deny(warnings)]; fast suite 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/BBAAv baseline; rbw-tf qualify-fast green; tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.moriah.sh now passes manifest verification (responds with vessel-exists precondition surfacing per BBAAs as expected). eba8f52f  ₢BBAAs    RCG named-string repair on the prior notch's BURE_CONFIRM bypass introduction. Operator flagged the 6 sites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs that read `&[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` as RCG-violating magic strings (per Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md the codebase requires named string constants for env-var keys and protocol values). Prior precedent already existed: rbtdrp_pristine.rs:106-107 had module-private `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` + `const RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` consumed at line 1030 — same primitive, but module-prefixed and not shared. Adding a second module's worth of magic strings would have created a duplicate-constants smell across rbtdrp_/rbtdrc_, so consolidated by extraction: defined `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY: &str = "BURE_CONFIRM"` and `pub const RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP: &str = "skip"` in rbtdri_invocation.rs (the natural home: rbtdri_invoke_global is the primitive that propagates env-pairs to tabtargets, and rbtdri_invocation.rs already exports RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR and RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY using the same pattern). Updated rbtdrc_crucible.rs (6 sites) and rbtdrp_pristine.rs (1 site at line 1025; was line 1030 before the dead-constant deletion) to import and use the new shared constants. Removed the rbtdrp_-private duplicates (4 lines including the docstring) since the shared rbtdri_ versions now serve as the single source of truth across the rbtd crate. Verification: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (0.51s, no warnings); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/this-officium-prior-notch baseline; grep `\"BURE_CONFIRM\"\|\"skip\"` confirms no remaining magic-string sites of these values in src/. Out of scope this notch (would expand the patch surface beyond what the operator review identified): broader audit of other env-var literals in rbtd/src/ such as RBTDRI_IFRIT_BINARY-pattern strings used elsewhere, and possible pull-up of rbtdri_invocation.rs's BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR alongside other dispatch-side conventions (BURD_BUK_DIR, BURD_TOOLS_DIR keys consumed by spawned tabtargets) into a unified env-key constants table. Discovery recipe for residual magic strings: `grep -rn '\\"[A-Z_]\\+\\"' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/ | grep -v '//\\|test_'` to enumerate uppercase string literals that look like env keys or protocol tokens, then evaluate per RCG whether each warrants extraction — deferred since today's empirical loop is the active priority. 3eda4229  ₢BBAAs    Two orthogonal fixes for the BBAAs onboarding-sequence empirical loop, landed together. (A) Three-line nudge in rbob_bottle.sh: at the three internal callsites (rbob_kludge:503, rbob_kludge_sentry:534, rbob_ordain:565) replace `rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"` with `BUZ_FOLIO="${RBRN_*_VESSEL}" rbfd_kludge` (and the ordain analog). Drops the now-dead `local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_*_VESSEL}"` plus its `test -d` guard at each site (existence check moves into rbfd's own `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, single source of truth for vessel resolution diagnostics). Solves kludge-tadmor blocker observed in today's empirical run: trace `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh: line 185: rbrv_list_capture: command not found` was symptom of `zrbfc_resolve_vessel(BUZ_FOLIO="tadmor")` falling through to listing branch because `tadmor` is the nameplate moniker (inherited from rbw-cKS dispatch into rbob_cli.sh process), not a vessel sigil — rbfd_kludge expected sigil/path. Bash assignment-prefix `VAR=value func` is the language's primitive for scoped env override and is symmetric with the dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec` (buz_zipper.sh:280) — same primitive at finer scope. Preserves BBAAs's universal-BUZ_FOLIO-contract design intent (handlers read BUZ_FOLIO uniformly across all rb*_, jjfp_, buhw_, rbgg_ modules; cli.sh furnishes also read BUZ_FOLIO for kindle); the rbob → rbfd internal calls just needed to honor the contract by setting BUZ_FOLIO to rbob's natural-derivative (RBRN_*_VESSEL sigil) for the inner call. After this patch, BUZ_FOLIO usage is uniform: dispatcher writes once via exec, callers may scope-override via assignment-prefix, all consumers read. Zero handler/dispatcher edits needed. (B) Strip --force flag from rbfl_jettison and rbfl_abjure plus their RBSIJ/RBSAA specs and 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs — origin trace: docket revision 260128-2116 for ₢APAAT (silks `add-image-delete-by-ark`) introduced 'Confirm before deletion or require --force flag' as freelance docket text never operator-authorized; subsequent reslating preserved it; alignment commit 525b7fac (`:A:` Feb 9 2026) pulled `(add confirmation/force)` into the spec; bd344f92 (Haiku sub-agent, same day) implemented z_force/z_skip_confirm/--force doc-param mechanically; 388dd454 carried it through the rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh extraction; 7167b795 (BBAAs) included `${2:-}` → `${1:-}` shift specifically because of --force. Removed: `local z_force="${1:-}"`, `buc_doc_param "--force" "..."`, `local z_skip_confirm=false` + `if test "${z_force}" = "--force"` branch, and the `if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"` wrapping around `buc_require` — `buc_require` runs unconditionally (single coherent path). The codebase already had a non-load-bearing-correct skip mechanism (`BURE_CONFIRM=skip` env var checked at buc_require:335-338, buc_command.sh) — --force was a redundant duplicate variant. Updated all 6 Rust orchestrator callsites in rbtdrc_crucible.rs (lines 2603, 2688, 2720, 2820, 2948, 3003) from `&[&hallmark, "--force"], &[]` to `&[&hallmark], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")]` — fixtures continue to auto-confirm via the existing env-var bypass; --force literal disappears from the entire rbk tree (verified via grep). Spec edits: RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:51-59 collapses 'Unless --force flag is provided' nested-step into unconditional 'Confirm Jettison' step; RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc:84-92 same shape. Verification: bash -n green on rbob_bottle.sh and rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile, 1.76s, no Rust warnings); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh green (tabtargets, RBW colophon registrations, generated context freshness all OK); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing) — no new failures introduced. Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Deferred follow-ups (separate notches when relevant): (i) source rbrv_regime.sh in zrbob_furnish to make rbrv_list_capture available in failure-listing branches reached from rbob processes (currently latent, surfaces only on resolution-failure paths and obscures real diagnostics); (ii) audit other Rust orchestrator/handbook callers passing positional args to BUZ_FOLIO-reading handlers — discovery recipe `grep -rn "rbtdri_invoke_global\|rbtdri_invoke_global_imprint" Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/` cross-referenced against zipper channel column for any handler with internal bash callers in rb*.sh modules. Caveat: scoped to the empirical failure trace and the unauthorized-flag scrub; if any handler outside this bug class has a similar internal-caller mismatch, will surface on the next empirical run as a new failure to diagnose.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.

## Heat ₣BB — rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (racing, 36/49)

**Paddock shape:** Constructing `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — third release-gate tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. Exists to catch silent first-build assumptions that slip through `rbw-tf`/`rbw-tr` because they tolerate accumulated state. Composed as a "gauntlet" TestSuite over 11 fixtures (enrollment-validation → pristine-lifecycle → canonical-establish → onboarding-sequence → regime-validation → regime-smoke → four-mode → tadmor → moriah → srjcl → pluml). Suite assembly landed at ₢BBAAg.

**Recent thread:** BBAAs swept folio passing across handlers (channel `""` → `param1`, read `${1:-}` → `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`); BBAAv promoted operation-name vocabulary into the manifest registry; BBAAg landed final gauntlet assembly.

## Next pace: ₢BBAA6 — yoke-domain-shift-all-vessels [rough]

**Silks fit:** Reasonable — captures the domain shift and the all-vessels iteration model. No rename suggested.

**Locked design (from docket):**
- `rbw-dY` channel: `param1` → `""` (no folio); iterates every `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/*/rbrv.env` and writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into each
- Primitive does **not** git commit; orchestrator (inscribe-reliquary case in onboarding-sequence) commits at end-of-case
- Frontispiece: `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels`
- Pre-validate reliquary stamp before touching any vessel; failure diagnostic names which vessel failed and which already wrote
- Bundled orphan cleanup: delete `rbev-busybox-airgap-negative-canary`, `rbev-busybox-native-verify`, `rbev-sentry-deb-airgap`, `rbev-ubu-safety`

**Done:** `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` passes; fast suite 26/26; `rbw-tf` green; `#![deny(warnings)]` clean; spec + tabtarget filename + zipper + frontispiece all consistent.

## Proposed approach

1. **Reshape the primitive** (RBSDY rewrite + handler in rbfl): wildcard-discover vessels, pre-validate stamp once, iterate writes with per-vessel diagnostic context; drop folio param entirely
2. **Sweep call surface**: zipper enrollment (channel `""`), tabtarget rename `DirectorYokesReliquaryInVessel.sh` → `DirectorYokesReliquaryAllVessels.sh`, remove `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` const + per-vessel call sites in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`, audit `RBTDRO_WITNESS_VESSEL_DIR` for wildcard impact
3. **Orphan cleanup** (independent): delete the four orphan `rbev-*` vessel directories
4. **Verify**: `rbtd-b` build, `rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast` 26/26, `rbw-tf`, `rbw-MG` regenerate context

**Strategy:** Sequential opus — multi-file but design-locked; needs judgment on diagnostic shape and witness-probe interaction. No parallelization gain.

Proceed?
I'll start with discovery — caller audit, current spec, current handler, current zipper enrollment, current tabtarget, and the failing test.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_yoke}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation writes `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»` into an ordain-path vessel's `rbrv.env` so that subsequent Cloud Build operations (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine) consume the named tool image set as GCB step images.
5	The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all ordain modes; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
6	Both arguments are validated before any file write: the stamp against GAR (all six tool packages must be present under the reliquary subtree), the vessel against its RBRV enrollment.
7	On validation failure the operation errors diagnostically without touching any file.
8	
9	The
10	{rbtr_director}
11	yokes a reliquary stamp into a vessel's regime:
12	
13	//axhopt_typed_parameter
14	{rbtr_director}
15	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
16	.. {rbbc_require}
17	`«VESSEL_SIGIL»` provided as folio (e.g., `rbev-sentry-deb-tether`)
18	.. {rbbc_require}
19	`«STAMP»` provided as first positional argument (e.g., `r260327172456`)
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Load Vessel* (CLI furnish, BUZ_FOLIO-gated):
23	.. Resolve `«VESSEL_SIGIL»` to vessel directory `${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/«VESSEL_SIGIL»`
24	.. {rbbc_require}
25	vessel directory contains `rbrv.env`
26	.. Source `rbrv.env` to populate `RBRV_*` variables
27	.. Kindle RBRV enrollment (no enforce — yoke writes `RBRV_RELIQUARY`,
28	which is a kindle constant with min=1 enrollment; pre-write enforce
29	would self-veto on marshal-zero state. The strict validator path is
30	`rbw-rvv` ValidateVesselRegime.)
31	
32	//axhos_step
33	. *Authenticate as
34	{rbtr_director}*:
35	.. Execute
36	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
37	.. {rbbc_store}
38	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
39	
40	//axhos_step
41	. *List Reliquary Packages in GAR*:
42	.. {rbbc_store}
43	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`)
44	.. {rbbc_store}
45	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-gar` (resolved by `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`)
46	.. {rbbc_call}
47	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[packages.list] for
48	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
49	/
50	{rbrr_gcp_region}
51	/
52	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
53	with `pageSize=1000`
54	.. {rbbc_require}
55	HTTP 200 response
56	.. Filter response package names to those starting with `{gar_reliquaries_namespace}/«STAMP»/`
57	.. Strip the subtree prefix to yield the bare tool-name set present in GAR
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Verify Tool Image Completeness*:
61	.. Expected tool name set: `gcloud`, `docker`, `alpine`, `syft`, `binfmt`, `skopeo` (per
62	{rbtgo_depot_inscribe})
63	.. {rbbc_require}
64	all six names present in the filtered list
65	.. {rbbc_fatal}
66	if any expected tool is missing — the error message names `«STAMP»`, the reliquary subtree path, and the specific missing tool(s); remediation points at
67	{rbtgo_depot_inscribe}
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Rewrite rbrv.env*:
71	.. Load the vessel's `rbrv.env` into memory, line by line
72	.. For each line matching `^RBRV_RELIQUARY=` — substitute with `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»`, preserving position
73	.. All other lines pass through unchanged
74	.. If no `RBRV_RELIQUARY=` line was found — append one under a `# Tool Image Reliquary` comment header
75	.. Write the composed content to a temp file, then atomically replace `rbrv.env` via rename
76	
77	//axhoc_completion
78	Successful execution writes `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»` into the vessel's `rbrv.env`; no GAR state is modified.
79	The
80	{rbtr_director}
81	commits the modified `rbrv.env` with their usual git workflow.
82

181	
182	  # Compute reliquary datestamp: r + YYMMDDHHMMSS
183	  local -r z_reliquary="r${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
184	  buc_info "Reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
185	
186	  # Submit inscribe as a Cloud Build job (docker pull/tag/push on GCB)
187	  zrbfl_inscribe_submit "${z_token}" "${z_reliquary}"
188	
189	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY}" "${z_reliquary}"
190	
191	  buc_success "Inscribe complete — reliquary ${z_reliquary} created"
192	  buc_info "Add RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary} to vessel rbrv.env files to use this reliquary"
193	}
194	
195	rbfl_yoke() {
196	  zrbfl_sentinel
197	
198	  local -r z_vessel="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
199	  local -r z_stamp="${1:-}"
200	
201	  buc_doc_brief "Yoke a reliquary stamp into a vessel's rbrv.env — validate both, then rewrite RBRV_RELIQUARY"
202	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil — folio (e.g., rbev-sentry-deb-tether)"
203	  buc_doc_param "stamp"  "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260327172456)"
204	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
205	
206	  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel sigil required (folio)"
207	  test -n "${z_stamp}"  || buc_die "Reliquary stamp required (arg 1)"
208	
209	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_vessel}"
210	  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
211	
212	  case "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" in
213	    conjure|bind|graft) ;;
214	    *)                  buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has unrecognized RBRV_VESSEL_MODE='${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}'" ;;
215	  esac
216	  buc_info "Vessel valid — mode=${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}, egress=${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}"
217	
218	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
219	  local z_token=""
220	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
221	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
222	
223	  buc_step "Validating reliquary stamp: ${z_stamp}"
224	  local -r z_rqy_subtree="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}/"
225	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
226	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_yoke_list"
227	
228	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
229	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List reliquary packages" "${z_list_infix}"
230	
231	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
232	  local -r z_present_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_present.txt"
233	
234	  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_rqy_subtree}" '
235	    .packages[]?.name
236	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
237	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
238	    | select(startswith($subtree))
239	    | ltrimstr($subtree)
240	  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_present_file}" \
241	    || buc_die "Failed to extract reliquary package names"
242	
243	  local z_present=()
244	  local z_line=""
245	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
246	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
247	    z_present+=("${z_line}")
248	  done < "${z_present_file}"
249	
250	  local -r z_expected=(
251	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
252	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
253	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
254	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
255	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
256	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
257	  )
258	
259	  local z_missing=""
260	  local z_tool=""
261	  for z_tool in "${z_expected[@]}"; do
262	    local z_found=0
263	    local z_i=""
264	    for z_i in "${!z_present[@]}"; do
265	      test "${z_present[$z_i]}" = "${z_tool}" || continue
266	      z_found=1
267	      break
268	    done
269	    case "${z_found}" in
270	      1) ;;
271	      *) z_missing="${z_missing}${z_missing:+, }${z_tool}" ;;
272	    esac
273	  done
274	
275	  test -z "${z_missing}" || buc_die "Reliquary stamp '${z_stamp}' not found in Depot — expected 6 tool images under ${z_rqy_subtree}; missing: ${z_missing}. Re-run tt/rbw-dI.DirectorInscribesReliquary.sh to mint a fresh reliquary, or verify the stamp spelling."
276	  buc_info "Reliquary valid — all 6 tool images present (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo)"
277	
278	  buc_step "Yoking ${z_vessel} to reliquary ${z_stamp}"
279	
280	  local z_rbrv_lines=()
281	  local z_rbrv_line=""
282	  while IFS= read -r z_rbrv_line || test -n "${z_rbrv_line}"; do
283	    z_rbrv_lines+=("${z_rbrv_line}")
284	  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
285	
286	  local -r z_tmp_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_rbrv.env.new"
287	  : > "${z_tmp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to create ${z_tmp_file}"
288	
289	  local z_wrote=0
290	  local z_j=""
291	  for z_j in "${!z_rbrv_lines[@]}"; do
292	    case "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" in
293	      RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)
294	        printf 'RBRV_RELIQUARY=%s\n' "${z_stamp}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
295	          || buc_die "Failed to write RBRV_RELIQUARY line to ${z_tmp_file}"
296	        z_wrote=1
297	        ;;
298	      *)
299	        printf '%s\n' "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
300	          || buc_die "Failed to write line to ${z_tmp_file}"
301	        ;;
302	    esac
303	  done
304	
305	  case "${z_wrote}" in
306	    1) ;;
307	    *) printf '\n# Tool Image Reliquary\nRBRV_RELIQUARY=%s\n' "${z_stamp}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
308	         || buc_die "Failed to append RBRV_RELIQUARY to ${z_tmp_file}" ;;
309	  esac
